memory_candidates.content: Status transition enforcement in the agent system is entirely documentation-driven (Claude-facing markdown instructions), not programmatic. The update-task-status.sh script performs no transition validation -- it only does idempotency checks and atomic file updates. All enforcement comes from Allowed Statuses tables in skill-orchestrator, checkpoint-gate-in, and per-command GATE IN sections.

When simplifying enforcement rules across a large markdown-based agent system, the most effective pattern is: (1) identify the canonical implementation first (skill-planner already had the permissive model), (2) use it as a template for all other files, (3) copy parent files to extension core mirrors last using cp + diff verification.

active_projects.completion_summary: Replaced forward-only status transition model with permissive terminal-state-only blocking across 21 files. Any command now runs from any non-terminal status; only completed, abandoned, and expanded block transitions.

memory_candidates.content: CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 only triggers fork when subagent_type is OMITTED from the Agent/Task tool call. Skills that explicitly specify subagent_type are unaffected by this env var.

context: fork (skill frontmatter) and CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 (env var) solve opposite problems. context: fork creates isolation, while FORK_SUBAGENT creates inheritance. They are complementary, not alternatives.

active_projects.completion_summary: Resolved context: fork documentation inconsistency across system-overview.md, thin-wrapper templates, and creation guides; created fork-patterns.md reference guide with decision matrix and cache sharing mechanics
